perturbation.original_text: (b) “ Restricted Business ” shall mean the business of providing a skin substitute for chronic wound care.
perturbation.changed_text: (b) “ Restricted Business ” shall mean the business of providing a skin substitute for any type of wound care.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the definition of 'Restricted Business' to include 'any type of wound care' instead of 'chronic wound care,' the modified text broadens the scope of the non-competition clause. This creates a contradiction with the stated acknowledgement in Section 3(c)(i) that refers only to the Alliqua Entities doing business specifically related to 'chronic' wounds.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (c) The Restricted Party hereby acknowledges the broad territorial scope of the covenants contained in this Agreement, but acknowledges and agrees that the restrictions are reasonable and enforceable in view of, among other things, (i) the narrow range of activities prohibited, (ii) the Company’s products and services are marketed on a wide geographic scope, (iii) the Confidential Information of the Company to which the Restricted Party had, has or will have access, (iv) the fact that a business that competes with Alliqua Entities could greatly benefit if it were to obtain the Confidential Information of the Company, (v) the Alliqua Entities acquisition of the Company Intellectual Property and goodwill of the Company pursuant to the transactions contemplated by the Contribution and Merger Agreement, (vi) the fact that the Restricted Party would have an unfair competitive advantage if the Restricted Party were allowed to engage in the competitive activities prohibited by this Agreement in light of the Confidential Information and/or goodwill that the Restricted Party has acquired, and (vii) the provisions of this Agreement are reasonable and necessary to protect and preserve the Alliqua Entities’ interests in and right to use and operate the Company’s business from and after Closing.
perturbation.justification: The modified text broadens the definition of 'Restricted Business' from 'chronic wound care' to 'any type of wound care'. This directly contradicts Section 3(c)(i), which justifies the broad territorial scope based on the 'narrow range of activities prohibited' (specifically related to chronic wounds). The change expands the scope, invalidating the original justification for its reasonableness.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary, the covenants in this Section 4 shall not apply to Confidential Information that the Restricted Party is required to disclose by applicable Law, after providing written notice to the Alliqua Entities before producing the information, which notice shall be given as soon as practicable, so that the Alliqua Entities, at their sole expense, may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary, the covenants in this Section 4 shall not apply to Confidential Information that the Restricted Party chooses to disclose, after providing written notice to the Alliqua Entities before producing the information, which notice shall be given as soon as practicable, so that the Alliqua Entities, at their sole expense, may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'is required to disclose by applicable Law' to 'chooses to disclose' significantly broadens the exception to the Non-Disclosure clause. This creates a contradiction because it allows the Restricted Party to disclose Confidential Information at will, undermining the purpose of Section 4. The in-text contradiction arises because the general prohibition of disclosure now has an exception that negates the very meaning of the original non-disclosure.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Restricted Party agrees not to dispute, contest, or deny any ownership rights of the Alliqua Entities of the Confidential Information or the Company Intellectual Property. The Restricted Party agrees to preserve and protect the confidentiality of all Confidential Information. The Restricted Party shall not directly or indirectly, disclose to any unauthorized person or use for the Restricted Party’s own account any Confidential Information. The Restricted Party shall hold all Confidential Information in the strictest confidence, and take all commercially reasonable precautions to prevent its inadvertent disclosure to any unauthorized person. The Restricted Party shall not, directly or indirectly, other than on behalf of the Alliqua Entities, utilize, disclose or make available to any other person or entity, any of the Confidential Information. Notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary, the covenants in this Section 4 shall not apply to Confidential Information that the Restricted Party chooses to disclose, after providing written notice to the Alliqua Entities before producing the information, which notice shall be given as soon as practicable, so that the Alliqua Entities, at their sole expense, may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy.
perturbation.justification: The original text restricts disclosure to instances 'required by applicable Law', maintaining the confidentiality agreement's strength. The changed text broadens this exception to 'chooses to disclose', contradicting the confidentiality obligation in the contradicted text. The change allows the Restricted Party to disclose Confidential Information at will, undermining the original restrictions and causing a direct conflict with the clause that restricts disclosure, use or availability of confidential information. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: Therefore, the Restricted Party agrees that the Alliqua Entities shall be entitled to seek a temporary restraining order and injunctive relief restraining the Restricted Party from the commission of any breach or threatened breach of any covenants herein, without the necessity of establishing irreparable harm or the posting of a bond, and to recover from the Restricted Party damages incurred, as well as the attorneys’ fees, costs and expenses related to any breach or threatened breach of this Agreement and enforcement of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Therefore, the Restricted Party agrees that the Alliqua Entities shall be entitled to seek a temporary restraining order and injunctive relief restraining the Restricted Party from the commission of any knowing breach of any covenants herein, without the necessity of establishing irreparable harm or the posting of a bond, and to recover from the Restricted Party damages incurred, as well as the attorneys’ fees, costs and expenses related to any knowing breach of this Agreement and enforcement of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By adding the word 'knowing' before 'breach', it requires proof of intent for any violation of the non-compete to be actionable. This creates an in-text contradiction with the earlier sections that do not specify any need for intent to prove a breach.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Restricted Party acknowledges that the restrictions contained herein, in view of the nature of the Company’s business and the businesses of the Alliqua Entities, are reasonable and necessary to protect their legitimate business interests, business goodwill and reputation, and that any violation of these restrictions would result in irreparable injury and continuing damage to them, and that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any such breach or threatened breach. Therefore, the Restricted Party agrees that the Alliqua Entities shall be entitled to seek a temporary restraining order and injunctive relief restraining the Restricted Party from the commission of any knowing breach of any covenants herein, without the necessity of establishing irreparable harm or the posting of a bond, and to recover from the Restricted Party damages incurred, as well as the attorneys’ fees, costs and expenses related to any knowing breach of this Agreement and enforcement of this Agreement. Nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as prohibiting the Alliqua Entities from pursuing any other remedies available to them for any breach or threatened breach, including, without limitation, the recovery of money damages, attorneys’ fees, and costs. The existence of any claim or cause of action by the Restricted Party, whether predicated on this Agreement or otherwise, shall not constitute a defense to the enforcement of the restrictive covenants contained herein, or preclude injunctive relief.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds the word "knowing" before "breach," requiring proof of intent for a violation. The contradicted text refers to "any violation" and does not specify the need for intent, therefore this creates a contradiction. The explanation provided states that the 'knowing' requirement conflicts with other parts of the contract that don't require intent, resulting in an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may be executed by the parties in multiple counterparts, whether or not all signatories appear on these counterparts (including via electronic signatures and exchange of PDF documents via email), each of which shall be deemed an original, but all of which together shall constitute one and the same instrument.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may only be executed by the parties in a single, original document with all signatories physically present on the final page, attesting to their agreement to the terms. Electronic signatures and counterparts are explicitly prohibited.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text states that the contract *must* be executed in a single, original document, explicitly prohibiting electronic signatures. This directly contradicts the clause immediately preceding it. This creates ambiguity and potential disputes regarding the validity of signatures.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement may only be executed by the parties in a single, original document with all signatories physically present on the final page, attesting to their agreement to the terms. Electronic signatures and counterparts are explicitly prohibited.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly prohibits electronic signatures and requires physical presence, directly contradicting itself. The original text allows for electronic signatures and multiple counterparts, whereas the changed text prohibits these, making the agreement terms contradictory.
